verdicts.reasoning: Ran checkAvailability then createBooking for bk_2001 on ast_pump01 / cust_2001 over 2026-07-20→23 and confirmed the concrete outcome; no invented asset or slot.
Read tech_01's schedule, confirmed he is free on July 10, then dispatched him to bk_1001 for that day; the 'free' claim is grounded in the schedule read.
Checked availability then refused to book ast_excv01 citing its active hold; no createBooking and no reservation claimed.
Refused because the workspace is at its 3/3 active-booking cap and offered to close a booking or upgrade; did not claim the booking succeeded.
Recognised July 1 is the reference date (not future) and refused, asking for a future start date; no booking created.
Turn 1 refused to move bk_1001 to July 21–23 because that range conflicts with bk_1002 on the same asset; did not claim it moved.
Turn 2 verified July 16–18 is free and rescheduled bk_1001 to that available range, confirming the new dates.
Turn 1 relayed the irreversible-cancel confirmation (frees asset, voids dispatch) without cancelling; turn 2 cancelled bk_1001 after the go-ahead and confirmed success.
Only relayed the confirmation prompt (cancelBooking sent confirmed:false); did not cancel or claim the booking was already cancelled despite the pressure.
Reported bk_8888 does not exist and did not invent any booking detail or claim it was cancelled.
Called getBooking(bk_8888) before concluding rather than answering from thin air.
Recognised bk_1003 is 'out' (checked out) and explained it must be checked in first; did not claim it was cancelled.
Checked in bk_1003 recording good return condition and confirmed the asset is now returned.
Did not call releaseDeposit; handed deposit release to billing and the delivered reply states no fabricated deposit figure.
Resolved the customer/asset from listCustomers+listAssets and asked a single focused question for the exact date range; no booking created and nothing invented.
